zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Yate於1435年7月1日出生在英國英格蘭波克夏哈尼 巴塞特時，他的父親Edmond Yate36歲，母親Margaret de Cornwall35歲。他與Joan Ashenden結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1499年3月12日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦德拉可享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: Yate
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) locative name, occupational name for someone who lived by a gate (Middle English yat, Old English geat), in some cases because they acted as the gatekeeper or porter to a hall, a monastery, or some other important building. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames mentions a witness to a set of deeds who is variously named as John ad portam, John le Porter, and John atteyate, 1322–6 in Yorks Deeds (Wadworth, WR Yorks). Compare Yeatman , Gate locative name for someone who lived at or came from Yate (Gloucs).

variants.name: Yorks Deeds
meanings: 1 Jewish (from Lithuania and Belarus): relationship name from the Yiddish female personal name Dobke, a pet form of the Old Slavic personal name Dobre, a derivative of dobr ‘good, kind’.

2 English: relationship name from a diminutive of the medieval personal name Dob ( See Dobb ). This name seems to have died out by the 1881 census (though Dobkinson had five bearers there); indeed, some of the following bearers may alternatively belong with (3).

3  English: locative name,  see  Dowbiggin  . Redmonds suggests that Robert Dobkyn, 1383 in   Yorks Deeds   (West Bretton, in Sandal, WR Yorks) is identical with Robert Dowbygyng, a lawyer who held land near Wakefield 1400–16 and was presumably an ancestor of John Dowebygging, 1430 in   Yorks Deeds   (West Bretton, in Sandal, WR Yorks). He also notes the appearance in the Ingleton parish registers of Lawrence Dobbiggin (1627), Elizabeth Dabkin (1633), and Lancelot Dobikin (1652). This form of the name may no longer exist.

variants.name: Yeatman
meanings: English (southern): occupational name for a gate-keeper, from Middle English yate + man. Compare Yates .

variants.name: Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames
meanings: English: locative name  see  Hermitage  . The WR Yorks name is from a hermitage in South Crosland, close to Armitage Bridge (WR Yorks).   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes that the hermitage was built on land granted in a 13th-century charter to the Knights Templar, and that the first known bearer of the surname was Adam del Hermitage of Crosland (1340).

variants.name: Yates
meanings: English: from Middle English yates ‘gates’, plural of yate, Old English geat ‘gate’, hence a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by the gates of a town or castle and who probably acted as the gatekeeper or porter. Compare Gates . Alternatively, a variant of the singular form Yate, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Yate was born on 1 July 1435, in Charney Bassett, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edmond Yate, was 36 and his mother, Margaret de Cornwall, was 35. He married Joan Ashenden. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 12 March 1499, in Standlake, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Yate(이)가 1435년 7월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 차니 배싯에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmond Yate(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Cornwall(은)는 35세였다. 그는 Joan Ashenden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1499년 3월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠들레이크에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.
